title: campanula rotundifolia subsp. eurotundifolia
intro: Campanula rotundifolia subsp. eurotundifolia, also known as the Scottish harebell or bluebell, is a flowering plant species within the Campanula genus. It is a perennial plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair. Campanula rotundifolia subsp. eurotundifolia can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which are commonly used in herbal medicine to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
